name: White Bread with Poolish (scalable)
description: Simple white bread with poolish pre-ferment based on "Flour Water Salt Yeast" by Ken Forkish. Open crumb, crispy crust, straightforward. Optionally with corn, cheese, or chili.
source: Ken Forkish - Flour Water Salt Yeast

items.name: Instant yeast
items.notes: Or 3x the amount as fresh yeast (dissolve in a little water)

items.name: Water
items.notes: Exactly 41°C. With fresh yeast: approx. 90% at 41°C, remainder at 23°C

items.name: Instant yeast
items.notes: Or 3x the amount as fresh yeast (dissolve in the cooler water portion)

items.name: Vital wheat gluten
items.notes: Optional, per 1 kg flour. Add to the second half of the flour.

ingredientGroups.name: Optional mix-ins (fold in during stretch and folds)

items.name: Corn
items.notes: Canned, drained. Or kernels cut from the cob.

items.name: Cheese
items.notes: Coarsely grated or cubed. Gouda, cheddar, Gruyère.

items.name: Chili
items.notes: Fresh, seeded and finely chopped. Or 1-2 tsp chili flakes.

steps.name: Mix Poolish
steps.description:
{{ingredient:Bread flour}} flour and {{ingredient:Instant yeast}} yeast, mix together.
Add instant yeast directly to the flour; dissolve fresh yeast in a little water first.
Add {{ingredient:Water}} water (27°C), stir to combine.
Seal the container.

steps.name: Poolish fermentation
steps.description:
Let the poolish ferment for 12-14 hours at 18-21°C.
Processing window: 1-2 hours after maturity.
At 24°C room temperature, only a 1-hour window.

steps.name: Mix dough
steps.description:
{{ingredient:Bread flour}} flour, {{ingredient:Salt}} salt, and {{ingredient:Instant yeast}} yeast — mix together in a large container.
Dissolve the poolish in {{ingredient:Water}} warm water (41°C) and work into the flour.

Target dough temperature: 23-24°C.
Seal the container.

steps.name: Stretch and fold
steps.description:
Perform 2-3 sets of stretch and folds every 20 minutes during the first hour.
Technique: https://youtu.be/CQHuWDEo3SA

If using optional mix-ins: fold them in during the last set.

steps.name: Fold in mix-ins
steps.description:
Spread optional mix-ins (corn, cheese, chili) evenly over the dough and incorporate with one final fold.
Do not overwork — just fold in.
Combine to taste; you don't need to use all of them at once.

steps.name: Bulk fermentation
steps.description:
Let the dough continue rising until roughly 2.5x its original size.
Poke test: press 2-3 cm deep.
- Indentation slowly/partially springs back = ready
- Indentation springs back immediately = not ready yet
- Indentation stays = over-proofed

steps.name: Divide and shape
steps.description:
Flour the work surface.
Gently turn the dough out of the container.
Divide into {{yield:amount}} halves.
Pre-shape each half into a round, building surface tension.
Dust lightly with flour, place into floured proofing baskets.
Slip into plastic bags.

Technique: https://youtu.be/MPdedk9gJLQ

steps.name: Final proof
steps.description:
Let the dough pieces proof in the baskets for 1 hour.
Use the poke test to check readiness.

steps.name: Preheat Dutch oven
steps.description:
Place the Dutch oven in the oven.
Heat to 245°C.
Preheat for at least 45 minutes.
Optionally verify temperature with an oven thermometer.

steps.name: Bake bread
steps.description:
Carefully place the dough into the hot Dutch oven.
Bake 30 minutes with the lid on.
Remove the lid and bake another 30 minutes until a dark brown crust forms.

Technique: https://youtu.be/rtoMhY0vS9E

hold.retrieveAction: Take loaf {n} out of the fridge, straight into the hot Dutch Oven

steps.name: Cooling
steps.description:
Remove the loaves from the Dutch oven.
Cool on a wire rack or standing upright.
Do NOT lay flat on the countertop (the bottom will get soggy).
Wait at least 20 minutes before slicing.

options.name: Freeze baked bread
options.description: Let the bread cool completely, slice it, and freeze in freezer bags with as little air as possible. Take individual slices from the freezer and toast directly.

shelfLife.qualityNotes: Toasted, almost like fresh. Untoasted, slightly chewy after thawing.

reactivationSteps.name: Toast slices
reactivationSteps.description: Put frozen slices directly into the toaster. Do not thaw first.

options.name: Refrigerator retard (shaped dough)
options.description: Place the shaped dough piece in its proofing basket into the refrigerator instead of proofing at room temperature. Bake the next day.

shelfLife.qualityNotes: Often even better than baked right away: more flavor from the longer fermentation, better crust. Best results within 12-18 hours.

reactivationSteps.name: Take dough out of refrigerator
reactivationSteps.description:
Take the dough piece out of the refrigerator.
Let it acclimatize at room temperature for 45-60 minutes.
Use the poke test to check readiness.

reactivationSteps.name: Preheat Dutch oven
reactivationSteps.description:
Heat the Dutch oven in the oven to 245°C.
Preheat for at least 45 minutes.
Start in parallel with the acclimatization.

reactivationSteps.name: Bake
reactivationSteps.description:
Place the dough into the hot Dutch oven.
30 minutes with the lid on, 30 minutes without.
Chilled dough often has even better oven spring.

reactivationSteps.name: Cooling
reactivationSteps.description: Cool on a wire rack or standing upright. At least 20 minutes.

notes.title: Recognizing poolish maturity
notes.content: The poolish is ready when bubbles pop on the surface every few seconds. Processing window: 1-2 hours after 12 hours of maturation. At 24°C room temperature, only a 1-hour window. Never wait longer than 14 hours.

notes.title: Water temperature
notes.content: Poolish: exactly 27°C. Main dough: exactly 41°C. Target dough temperature after mixing: 23-24°C. With fresh yeast, split the water: approx. 90% at 41°C, remainder at 23°C. Dissolve the yeast in the cooler water.

notes.title: Folding technique
notes.content: 2-3 sets of stretch and folds every 20 minutes during the first hour. Then leave the dough undisturbed until it reaches 2.5x its original size. Technique: https://youtu.be/CQHuWDEo3SA

notes.title: Shaping technique
notes.content: Ease the dough out of the container using a floured edge. Build surface tension during pre-shaping by dragging across the work surface. When placing into the basket, keep the bottom side down — do not twist the dough. Technique: https://youtu.be/MPdedk9gJLQ

notes.title: Poke test (proofing check)
notes.content:
Press 2-3 cm deep.
- Indentation slowly/partially springs back = dough is ready
- Indentation springs back immediately = not ready yet
- Indentation stays completely = over-proofed

notes.title: Baking
notes.content: Preheat Dutch oven for at least 45 min at 245°C. 30 min with lid (steam), 30 min without lid (crust). Place the second loaf in the refrigerator 20 min before the first is done. Technique: https://youtu.be/rtoMhY0vS9E

notes.title: After baking
notes.content: Cool on a wire rack or standing upright. Do NOT lay flat on the countertop (condensation makes the bottom soggy).

notes.title: Optional mix-ins
notes.content: Corn, cheese, and/or chili are folded in during the last stretch and fold. Use individually or in combination. Do not overwork — just fold in. Cheese melts during baking and creates pockets; corn caramelizes slightly.
